messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: What are the presets in use for Jamaica currently
messages.content_html: What are the presets in use for Jamaica currently

messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: I’m now in Jamaica, in the Portmore area. I have with me two new Seeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeed T1000-e units, bought a week or so ago (from Pi Hut, UK) as T1000-E for LoRaWAN (not the similar T1000-E for Meshtastic). These new ones seem to me to be a bit more stable than the MT variant, which could be because they’re more recent than my T1000-E for Meshtastic which I’ve had so much trouble with back in London recently on MC). These are set as MeshCore companions. There's an additional Heltec T114 attached to a USB solar panel resting on a metal sunroof at the front door, set as a MeshCore repeater. These three are all set to New Zealand (Narrow) preset, which is today’s guess as to what Jamaica can use. There’s nobody here but me, it seems. I’ll try a different preset tomorrow. I have other nodes on Meshtastic, which are set to Australia / New Zealand, which might be similar or might not, who knows. Again, nobody here but me on those settings.

messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: Yesterday my two T1000e companions and one T114 solar repeater were set to the preset New Zealand (Narrow) Today I’ve just set them all to Australia
messages.content_html: Yesterday my two T1000e companions and one T114 solar repeater were set to the preset New Zealand (Narrow) Today I’ve just set them all to Australia

messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: Day three, they’re all on USA/Canada (Recommended)
messages.content_html: Day three, they’re all on USA/Canada (Recommended)

messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: I think during the day I will set all the gear back to AU915-928 (AU915 / channel plan ID 5), rather than US902-918 (US915 / channel plan id 2), as I think upon further timewasting reading, this is the more appropriate region setting for Jamaica
messages.content_html: I think during the day I will set all the gear back to AU915-928 (AU915 / channel plan ID 5), rather than US902-918 (US915 / channel plan id 2), as I think upon further timewasting reading, this is the more appropriate region setting for Jamaica

messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: it's afternoon here in Jamaica and I’ve set all my mesh radios back to AU915 – specifically, the New Zealand (Narrow) preset, but … I’ve taken a walk down the street a bit now and I’m having no success even getting to the other node back at the house we’re staying at, so more experiments are needed if I get the time – for now though, I’ll have to try something other than New Zealand (Narrow) next
messages.content_html: it's afternoon here in Jamaica and I’ve set all my mesh radios back to AU915 – specifically, the New Zealand (Narrow) preset, but … I’ve taken a walk down the street a bit now and I’m having no success even getting to the other node back at the house we’re staying at, so more experiments are needed if I get the time – for now though, I’ll have to try something other than New Zealand (Narrow) next

messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: I’ve now set my MeshCore radios to the Australia preset, the one at the top of the preset list, next time I go out for a walk I'll test that and see how far away I can reach
messages.content_html: I’ve now set my MeshCore radios to the Australia preset, the one at the top of the preset list, next time I go out for a walk I'll test that and see how far away I can reach

messages.author: u0421793
messages.text: I’ve erased and reflashed the T114 and pair of new T1000-e over to Meshtastic. This is not a reflection on MeshCore being bad or anything, they both worked out about identically in my ‘walk down the road until it disappears’ tests the other day, on Australia preset (which was just marginally better than the NZ Narrow, hardly anything in it though). Basically I can reach halfway down the street. No further. Almost identically with identical gear on MT and MC both on Australia not-edge/narrow but normal preset on Long Fast and what I presume is the equiv of Long Fast in MeshCore. They’re both the same performance for me here. The reason for moving over to MT is simply that it’s not appropriate to have a ‘base station’ or repeater node, tied to one place – it ended up being useless, as due to the movement of the people we always ended up being in places the repeater wasn’t and had no chance of reaching (because it can only reach to halfway down the street). With Meshtastic, among us it worked well because we were all together wherever we ended up (I say ‘we’. I was connecting/disconnecting into all the MT devices myself on my one iPhone, and half the time I couldn’t because the Bluetooth getting in screen just said too many retries for hours, which is useless).
